items.description: Leading the automation process implementing RPA solutions. Developing ERP integrations with custom solutions. Performing customizations in the ERP according to the company's business logic.
Responsible for developing web solutions in the financial and real estate sector. Developing internal code conventions according to best programming practices. Leading the Backend development team.
Redesign and maintenance of white and web application in the educational sector. Teaching programming classes to senior students. Leading the Science and Technology committee of the institution.
Developed cost simulation application for construction materials. Managed and provided technical support to accounting and billing software. Implemented communication management and teamwork tools.

Projects.title: Projects
Projects.description: My most exciting and creative projects. Each project is the result of my dedication and passion for programming, and I'm delighted to share them with you. Discover how I transform ideas into digital realities. Explore, inspire and create with my software projects!

About.title: About me
About.p1: I am passionate about solving problems, whether it's building applications from scratch or uncovering the hidden logic behind data.
About.p2: Attention to detail, research, and curiosity allow me to see what others might miss.
About.p3: I believe technology is only useful if it makes people's lives easier; that’s why I put as much enthusiasm into writing clean code as I do into collaborating and communicating effectively with my team.

Stack.title: Technologies
Stack.description: In my journey through the world of web development, I have cultivated experience and skills in a variety of technologies. My technology stack includes:

meta.title: Cristian Orrego Dev 👨‍💻 | Digital services focused on business outcomes
meta.description: I design and build corporate websites, e-commerce platforms, and custom software to help you generate more opportunities, sell better, and run operations with less friction. AI is integrated only when it adds real value.
meta.keywords: web development services, corporate website, landing page, ecommerce development, custom software, AI automation, next.js development

hero.title: Corporate websites, e-commerce, and custom software to sell more and operate better.
hero.subtitle: Every project is built around business goals: strategic clarity, user experience, and technical execution. AI is added when it improves conversion, support, or automation—not as a trend.

painPoints.title: If this sounds familiar, there is a clear improvement opportunity.
painPoints.subtitle: Before talking about tech, we start with concrete blockers that hurt revenue, trust, or efficiency.
items: Your business does not convey trust online, making it hard to explain what you offer.
You get traffic, but your website does not convert into leads or sales consistently.
You are selling, but operations are still manual and dependent on chat apps or spreadsheets.
Your team loses time on repetitive tasks that could be automated.
You do not have a solid digital foundation to scale campaigns or add AI with purpose.

services.title: Three service lines, three types of results.
services.subtitle: Each service is built for a specific business context with concrete deliverables.

items.title: Corporate website and landing pages
items.forWho: For professionals, personal brands, and service companies
items.strategicResult: Turn your website into a commercial asset that builds authority and generates qualified opportunities.
items.problem: When your digital presence does not reflect your real value, opportunities are lost before the first sales conversation.
items.solution: I design a website focused on authority, clear messaging, and lead generation with a structure built to convert.
deliverables: Content architecture and clear value proposition
Baseline technical SEO and optimized performance
benefits: Clearer commercial message in under 10 seconds
aiOptions: Lead qualification chat based on intent
Initial response assistant for frequent inquiries

items.title: E-commerce
items.forWho: For brands selling physical or digital products
items.strategicResult: Build a reliable, scalable, and more predictable digital sales channel with lower operational friction.
items.problem: Having an online store is not enough when buying journeys are confusing or operations consume too much time.
items.solution: I implement e-commerce systems focused on conversion, clear catalogs, reliable payments, and commercial automation.
deliverables: Baseline automations for marketing and post-sale
benefits: Simpler and more trustworthy buying process
Less operational friction in daily execution
Foundation ready to scale campaigns and channels
aiOptions: Automated pre and post-sale support for repetitive questions

items.title: Custom software
items.forWho: For companies with manual processes or fragmented systems
items.strategicResult: Regain operational control through connected systems that reduce manual dependency and support sustainable growth.
items.problem: When operations rely on repetitive tasks and disconnected tools, growth becomes expensive and messy.
items.solution: I build internal systems and automations designed around your real operation—not the other way around.
deliverables: Integrations with APIs, ERP, or existing tools
Phased evolution to reduce delivery risk
benefits: Less manual work in critical workflows
Higher operational control in one place
aiOptions: Internal assistants for fast information lookup

ai.title: AI without hype
ai.subtitle: First we stabilize your digital foundation. Then we evaluate where AI can save time, improve decisions, or increase conversion with measurable impact.

yes: There are repetitive workflows with enough volume.
There is minimum reliable information to train or parametrize rules.
The business objective is clear and measurable.
no: AI is requested as decoration to appear innovative.
There is no defined process or internal ownership.
The digital foundation still has structural issues.

items.question: How long does a project take?
items.answer: It depends on scope. A landing page can be delivered in a few weeks, while custom software requires longer phases. A roadmap with incremental deliveries is defined from day one.

items.question: How much maintenance is required?
items.answer: The goal is to start from a maintainable base. Then we define a support model according to your needs: one-off improvements, monthly evolution, or ongoing support.

items.question: Can we start small and scale later?
items.answer: Yes. In fact, that is the recommended path: prioritize critical scope, validate with users, and evolve in stages to reduce risk.

items.question: Does the client own the code and assets?
items.answer: Yes. Ownership of assets and access is defined in the agreement from the beginning to avoid unnecessary dependency.

items.question: When does it make sense to add AI?
items.answer: When there is a repetitive, measurable problem with minimum data to operate. If the digital foundation is not ready, we strengthen that first.

items.question: What information do you need for a quote?
items.answer: Project goal, user type, current process, urgency, and references. That is enough to build a realistic initial scope.

closing.title: Ready to turn your idea into a solution that moves the business?
closing.subtitle: Start with a quick conversation. I will suggest the best path for your current context without unnecessary complexity.
